content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN such that Ai=iA_i = iAi​=i.

In one operation, Chef can pick any two elements of the array, delete them from AAA, and append either their bitwise XOR or their bitwise OR to AAA.

Note that after each operation, the length of the array decreases by 111.

Let FFF be the final number obtained after N−1N-1N−1 operations are made. You are given an integer XXX, determine if you can get F=XF=XF=X via some sequence of operations.

In case it is possible to get F=XF = XF=X, print the operations too (see the section Output format for more details), otherwise print −1-1−1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing two space-separated integers NNN and XXX.


Output Format
For each test case, output −1-1−1 if it is impossible to get XXX in the end.
Otherwise print N−1N-1N−1 lines denoting the operations.


On each line, print



1 x y1\ x\ y1 x y if you want replace xxx and yyy with x OR yx\ OR\ yx OR y.
2 x y2\ x\ y2 x y if you want replace xxx and yyy with x XOR yx\ XOR\ yx XOR y.

Note that xxx and yyy are the elements of the array at the time of applying the operation, and if either of them is not present in the array you will receive a WA verdict.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The operations are as follows:

Initially, the array is A=[1,2,3]A = [1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3]
The first move removes 111 and 333 and appends their XORXORXOR, making the array A=[2,2]A = [2, 2]A=[2,2]
The second move removes 222 and 222 and appends their OROROR, making the array A=[2]A = [2]A=[2]

Test case 222: It can be shown that no sequence of operations can achieve F=2F = 2F=2 in this case.
Test case 333: The operations are as follows:

Initially, the array is A=[1,2,3,4]A = [1, 2, 3, 4]A=[1,2,3,4]
After the first move, the array is A=[3,4,3]A = [3, 4, 3]A=[3,4,3]
After the second move, the array is A=[4,3]A = [4, 3]A=[4,3]
After the third move, the array is A=[7]A = [7]A=[7]
